Create the Building Frame.Instructions: Level the framework strongback and mark station locations
Align all molds on the strongback with station locatonis
using a centerline string making sure molds are vertical and centerline is plumb
Mount the station molds onto the strongback at the specified intervals

Attach transom panels.Instructions: Cut bow and stern transoms from plywood using the provided templates
Mark horizontal lines at sheer and side/bottom locations and mark vertical line at the center of each transom
Level and square the building frame
Screw the bow and stern transoms to the building frame making sure they are square and level and that center lines align with center string

Cut Hull Panels.Instructions: Cut two side panels and two bottom panels from plywood
Mark reference lines on all parts
Carefully cut a 45 degree bevel through 1/2 the thickness of each panel on the inboard edges of panels that touch each other. Do not bevel the edge of the side panels that forms the sheer

Build Cradle.Instructions: We need to specify how to do this

Assemble and tack hull.Instructions: Staple the bottom planks together along the centerline, then hang the bottoms over the mold being careful to align fore/aft and side to side with the frame
Staple one side to each bottom plank
Wire the side and bottom panels to the transoms

Glue Up Tacked Hull.Instructions: Apply thickened epoxy to all of the external joints and dab epoxy into the internal joints approximately every 6 inches DO NOT GET GLUE ON THE STAPLES
Trim sides and bottom to length

Fill and Fair Hull.Instructions: Fill and fair outside sides and fill holes and imperfections in the sides and transoms

Epoxy and Glass Hull.Instructions: Sand epoxy, and glass the exterior

Fillet and tape interior.Instructions: Carefully mark 10 inch long level lines on the outside of the hull and across one transom
Lift the boat from the frame install level and square on cradle
With the hull on cradle, transfer level lines to the inside of the boat
Lay fiberglass tape over the fillets (Note: MAKE SURE THE BOAT IS LEVEL AND NOT TWISTED because once the fillets dry the shape cannot be changed)

Cut Sheer and Transom.Instructions: Mark a boat centerline along the full length of the interior of the hull and match to the centerlines on the transoms
Cut the sheers and the transoms
Bevel the transoms according to the bevel provided

Sand and Epoxy Interior.Instructions: Apply epoxy to interior (Use WestSystem 50x), apply with roller and tip.

Forward Knee.Instructions: If a forward knee/mast step is not provided to you, use templats to mark and cut forward knee parts
Bevel the cut parts to the angles provided and cut half circles for limber hole
Sand, round edges, and seal with epoxy
Mark a centerline on the forward knee to help with installing
Trial fit the forward knee until the part fits square and level and the mast will be vertical or slightly aft-leaning

Install Forward Knee.Instructions: Install the forward knee aligning with the center of the boat and square and level

Build Transom Knee.Instructions: Use template to mark and cut an after transom knee.
Mark a centerline on the part
Round edges except the edge that joins to the after transom
Sand and seall the part with epoxy

Install Transom Knee.Instructions: Install the after transom knee aligning the center line on the transom center line and the bottom center line
Fillet and glass the transom knee and forward knee joints

Build Daggerboard Trunk.Instructions: Using the pattern, cut two daggerboard sides from 1/4 plywood
Cut two daggerboard spacers 12 inches in length and 1 1/2 by 5/8
Using a jig, glue the spacers to one side
Precoat the inside of the plywood sides and the spacers with epoxy
Using a jig, glue the second side to the first with the spacers on the interior
Sand and coat exterior with epoxy

Install Daggerboard Trunk.Instructions: Use the jig provided to cut through the boat bottom at the location specified
Slide the daggerbaroard trunk into the hole, brace it plumb and square and leaving about 10 inches above the bottom of the boat
Mark the trunk both where it meets the hull and 9 inches from where it meets the hull on the aft side of the trunk
Remove the daggerboardtrunk and cut the trunk top to be square and plumb to the 9 inch mark
Epoxy the daggerbaord trunk in place aligning the mark you made with the trunk meets the hull and making sure the trunk is plumb and square side to side.
Cut, mark, bevel and install the lower trunk supports

Install Daggerboard Trunk.Assembly Drawing File: SGH: I think this part will be prone to failing at the glue joint. We can create a router jig that automates the cutting of the slot in plywood.

Install Daggerboard Trunk.Instructions: Cut, mark,and install upper trunk supports
Put the thwart and cover support aside for installation later

Thwart Support.Instructions: Using patterns, cut two halves of the thwart support and glue to form one part.
Thwart Support.Assembly Drawing File:
SGH: I think this part will be prone to failing at the glue joint. We can create a router jig that automates the cutting of the slot in plywood. 
Also, we might group this step with the trunk assembly

Thwart Support.Instructions: Cut a 1" radius on the forward corners

Cut Thwart.Instructions: Mark the thward location using the thwart support to define the height. Install the thwart level to the boat and in contact with the support
Using a pattern, cut a thwart
Bevel the thwart to the required angle and verify the fit
Cut the slot in the thwart for the daggerboard

Install Thwart.Instructions: Attach thwart to trunk and cleats with countersunk screws
Install Thwart.Assembly Drawing File: SGH: We haven't designed the claat that attaches to the hull yet. Once we do, we'll need to mod the thwart template.

Deck panel.Instructions: Cut the plywood deck to the pattern

Deck Support.Instructions: Need to work out the process
Deck Support.Assembly Drawing File: SGH: As designed, the deck support has many parts and some compound miters. We should consider simplifying this design.

Prepare Gunwales.Instructions: Cut four 9 foot strips of 1 1/2" x 1/2" douglas fir. Ensure grain orientation runs with the 1 1/2" dimension
On two strips, rout a 1/4" roundover on two edges of a 1 1/2" face

Build Quarter Knees.Instructions: Use templates to mark and cut quarter knees.
Bevel the quarter knees in two dimensions
Round edges except the edge that joins to the after transom
Sand and seal the part with epoxy
